zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Eyre於1445年之後出生在英國林可爾史爾時，她的父親Robert Eyre II8577歲，母親Elizabeth FitzWilliam8573歲。她於1459年與Thomas Meverell在英國英格蘭德比夏爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1483年在英國英格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: Eyre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: This name was that of a historically prominent family in Galway (Ireland) since the 1650s.

variants.name: Ayer
meanings: English and Scottish: from Middle English eir, eyr ‘heir’ (Anglo-Norman French heyr, Old French (h)eir, Latin heres). Forms such as Richard le Heyer were frequent in Middle English, denoting a man who was well known to be the heir to the main property in a particular locality, either one who had already inherited or one with great expectations.
English: from the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Aier (ancient Germanic Agihari).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Eyre was born after 1445, in Lincolnshire, England, her father, Robert Eyre II, was 8577 and her mother, Elizabeth FitzWilliam, was 8573. She married Thomas Meverell in 1459, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1483, in England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Eyreが1445年以後にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert Eyre IIは8577歳，母Elizabeth FitzWilliamは8573歳であった。1459年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアでThomas Meverellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1483年にイギリスイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Eyre(이)가 1445년 이후 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Eyre II(은)는 8577세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth FitzWilliam(은)는 8573세였다. 그녀는 1459년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 Thomas Meverell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1483년 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Eyre родилась после 1445 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Eyre II было 8577 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth FitzWilliam, 8573. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Meverell в 1459 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1483 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания.
